_comment: HAND-AUTHORED skills-lane supplement (Solo todo 809). NOT generated by compile-routing.mjs and never touched by it — edit by hand. Each case is a question whose uniquely right route is a mirrored skill (service `skills` in catalog/manifest.json, ids `skills.<source>.<name>`). expected_cards use the `skills_<terminal-name>` form that eval/lib/grade.mjs's card normalizer maps to the skills service and matches against the catalog id's terminal op name. Loaded by eval/run-routing.mjs alongside the compiled routing-cases.json; graded as its own lane, never mixed into the legacy 338-case aggregate.
author: todo-809
retired_note: retiredCases are INERT (run-routing.mjs reads only `cases`): the 8 ll-family cases targeted the lumenloop-api onboarding skills + lumenloop-mcp-connect, retired from catalog exposure 2026-07-03 (Solo todo 825; deny-list-as-data in scripts/build-catalog.mjs). Their questions are direct-consumer transport tasks (key rotation, REST 401 onboarding, credit top-ups, connector install) that gateway callers cannot perform by design — no route in the exposed catalog is a right answer. Kept here for auditability; if the skills are ever re-exposed, move them back into `cases` and re-baseline the gate.

cases.question: I've never written a Stellar smart contract before — walk me through setting up the Rust project, the anatomy of a contract, and getting it built and deployed.
Give me the recommended patterns for a Soroban contract I'm developing: storage layout, require_auth usage, cross-contract calls, events, and error handling.
What testing strategy should I follow for my Soroban contract before mainnet — unit tests, fuzzing, property tests, fork tests, integration tests?
Give me a security review checklist for my Soroban contract — vulnerability classes to look for, tooling to run, and how to prep for an audit.
Set up a new Soroban project that uses OpenZeppelin Contracts for Stellar — Rust toolchain and Stellar CLI install, Cargo.toml dependencies, and the import conventions.
Make my Soroban contract upgradeable using OpenZeppelin's Upgradeable derive macro, including an atomic upgrade-and-migrate step and keeping storage keys compatible.
I want to add Ownable access control, a Pausable emergency switch, and a reentrancy guard to my Stellar contract using the OpenZeppelin library components.
Guide me through building a React frontend that connects the Freighter wallet, builds and signs a transaction, and invokes a Soroban contract from the browser with simulation and error handling.
Best-practice walkthrough for wiring Stellar Wallets Kit and passkey smart accounts into my Next.js app so users can sign with any wallet.
Walk me through building an indexer over Stellar chain data — which RPC methods vs Horizon endpoints to use, streaming, pagination, and where deep history comes from.
My analytics service needs account balances, transaction history, and contract events from Stellar — give me the query workflow and when to prefer RPC over Horizon.
Playbook for issuing a regulated stablecoin on Stellar: issuer/distributor account structure, authorization flags, clawback, trustline management, and exposing it to contracts through the SAC.
I want to sell my API to AI agents with per-request USDC micropayments on Stellar — should I use x402 or a payment channel, and how do I architect it for high-frequency traffic?
Give me the decision map for which Stellar standard applies to each part of my wallet build — anchor deposits, KYC, deep links, federation — and where to start with each.
Wire a Circom proving toolchain to Stellar for me — write the circuit, generate Groth16 proofs, and verify them on-chain in a Soroban contract with the BLS12-381 host functions.
Produce a full landscape report of the RWA tokenization sector on Stellar — the projects, grouped by category and region, plus the news and talks that explain the space.
Put together a dated, cited newsletter-style roundup of what's happened in Stellar DeFi over the last month — news, podcasts, events, published research.
I'm meeting the Blend team next week — build me a due-diligence dossier on the project: background, SCF funding history, conference talks, and similar projects.
My Stellar lending protocol needs a price oracle — find the right existing ecosystem project to integrate and route me to how to adopt it.
Before I submit my SCF application, position my idea against prior SCF submissions — what's already been funded in this area, what overlaps, and how do I sharpen the pitch?
Here's my draft announcement post about several Stellar projects — fact-check the project names and X handles, flag unsupported claims, and add citations before I publish.
I have an idea for a remittance product on Stellar but don't know where to start — which Stellar primitives fit, what prior art already exists, and what's my build path?
Vet my hackathon idea against existing Stellar projects and open SCF-funded RFPs, recommend an audit firm, and help me draft the SCF pitch.

retiredCases.question: My LumenLoop research call just failed with HTTP 402 — how do I check the account's remaining research budget and spend, and get the calls working again?
Walk me through topping up my LumenLoop prepaid credits with USDC on Stellar via x402, step by step, and how to request a bigger quota from an admin.
One of our LumenLoop API keys leaked — revoke it right now and mint labeled replacement keys with expiries so each agent gets its own credential.
I'm writing a production client for the LumenLoop REST API — generate a typed client from the OpenAPI spec and handle the response envelope, errors, and rate limits robustly.
From a plain HTTP script, how do I use the LumenLoop REST API's guest-tier read tools to browse the project directory, resolve names to slugs, and run semantic search?
First time calling the LumenLoop REST API and I'm getting a 401 — orient me on Bearer auth, the guest/partner tier ladder, rate limits, and a first smoke test.
Commission a metered server-side research report with citations over the LumenLoop corpus, then poll it and retrieve the result — what's the request_research workflow?
Set up the LumenLoop MCP connector in Claude Desktop for me and show me what its query tools cover — it isn't connecting right now.
